from Week 5 Discussion

Feb 11, 2025 5:31PM

Khalil Brown
Perseverance and Sacrifice in Eudora Welty’s A Worn Path
In Eudora Welty’s A Worn Path, symbolism plays a crucial role in illustrating the themes of perseverance and sacrifice. Phoenix Jackson, an elderly Black woman, embarks on a long and treacherous journey through the woods to obtain medicine for her ailing grandson. Along the way, Welty employs various symbols that highlight Phoenix’s resilience and the deep sacrifices she makes out of love. Two of the most significant symbols in the story are Phoenix’s name and the worn path itself, both of which reinforce the enduring nature of her struggle.
Phoenix’s name is a powerful symbol of rebirth, endurance, and unwavering determination. The phoenix, a mythical bird, is known for rising from its own ashes, symbolizing renewal and perseverance despite hardship. This directly parallels Phoenix Jackson’s journey, as she continuously overcomes obstacles that test her physical and mental strength. Even when she momentarily forgets the purpose of her journey upon reaching the doctor’s office, she regains her resolve, demonstrating her undying devotion: “My grandson. It was my memory had left me. There I sat and forgot why I made my long trip” (Welty, 1941/1980, p. 148). This moment underscores how exhaustion and age weigh on her, yet she persists for the sake of her grandson. Her resilience mirrors that of the phoenix, as she repeatedly undertakes this difficult path, never allowing hardship to deter her.
